posts.raw_content:
Guys, the news just dropped and it's massive: the UAE is officially leaving OPEC effective May 1st. And let me tell you, this is terrible news for the mullahs in Tehran.
Iran is already getting hammered from the recent war, their oil infrastructure is damaged, exports are crippled, and now one of the biggest and most capable Gulf producers is walking away from the cartel. The UAE has huge spare capacity and modern operations – they've been frustrated for years being told by OPEC (basically Saudi and the others) how much they can pump. Now they're free to crank up production on their own terms, flood the market with more oil, and keep prices from spiking too high for too long.
For Iran, this is brutal. Their economy depends heavily on high oil prices to survive sanctions and fund their terror proxies. With the UAE going independent and pumping more, it undercuts any chance Iran had of using oil as leverage or waiting out the pressure for prices to skyrocket. OPEC is looking weaker and more fractured than ever, which means less coordinated cuts that could have helped prop up Iran's broken finances.
This is what real leadership looks like – the UAE putting their own national interests first instead of playing along with a dysfunctional club that includes hostile actors. Iran’s regime is already on life support after the conflict, and this move just pulled the plug. Their economy is going to bleed out even faster now.
About damn time someone broke ranks. Sovereign nations shouldn't be chained to cartels that protect the weakest links.
posts.author: NSFW_PORN_ONLY
